### Custom Installation Directory Examples

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/README.md

Examples of setting custom installation directories for OpenCode.

```bash
OPENCODE_INSTALL_DIR=/usr/local/bin curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash
```

```bash
XDG_BIN_DIR=$HOME/.local/bin curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash
```

--------------------------------

### Request example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of an API request using cURL.

```bash
curl -X POST 'https://api.example.com/users' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"name": "John Doe", "email": "john@example.com"}'
```

--------------------------------

### Response example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of a JSON API response.

```json
{
  "id": "user_123",
  "name": "John Doe", 
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}
```

--------------------------------

### tmpdir with custom initialization

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Example demonstrating how to use a custom `init` function to perform setup and return extra data accessible via `tmp.extra`.

```typescript
await using tmp = await tmpdir<string>({
  init: async (dir) => {
    await Bun.write(path.join(dir, "file.txt"), "content")
    return "extra data"
  },
})
// Access extra data via tmp.extra
console.log(tmp.extra) // "extra data"
```

--------------------------------

### Quickstart Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/http-recorder/README.md

Example demonstrating how to use HttpRecorder's cassetteLayer to record or replay HTTP requests, with an option to force recording.

```ts
import { Effect } from "effect"
import { HttpClient, HttpClientRequest } from "effect/unstable/http"
import { HttpRecorder } from "@opencode-ai/http-recorder"

const program = Effect.gen(function* () {
  const http = yield* HttpClient.HttpClient
  const response = yield* http.execute(HttpClientRequest.get("https://api.example.com/users/1"))
  return yield* response.json
})

// Records if the cassette is missing, replays if it exists.
// In CI (CI=true) always replays — fails loudly on missing fixtures.
Effect.runPromise(program.pipe(Effect.provide(HttpRecorder.cassetteLayer("users/get-one"))))

// Force a refresh — always hits upstream and overwrites.
Effect.runPromise(program.pipe(Effect.provide(HttpRecorder.cassetteLayer("users/get-one", { mode: "record" }))))
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies (Linux)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Commands to install Node.js and a package on Linux using apt.

```bash
sudo apt install nodejs npm
npm install -g package-name
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/README.md

Command to install project dependencies using Bun.

```bash
bun install
```

--------------------------------

### Provider Facade Configuration Examples

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/llm/AGENTS.md

Examples demonstrating how to configure different LLM providers using their respective facades.

```ts
const openai = OpenAI.configure({ apiKey, baseURL })
const model = openai.responses("gpt-4o-mini")

const azure = Azure.configure({ resourceName, apiKey, apiVersion: "v1" })
const deployment = azure.responses("my-deployment")

const gateway = CloudflareAIGateway.configure({ accountId, gatewayId, gatewayApiKey, apiKey })
const proxied = gateway.model("openai/gpt-4o-mini")
```

--------------------------------

### Advanced configuration example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

An example of a JavaScript configuration object within an accordion.

```javascript
const config = {
  performance: { cache: true, timeout: 30000 },
  security: { encryption: 'AES-256' }
};
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies (Windows)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Commands to install Node.js and a package on Windows using Chocolatey.

```powershell
choco install nodejs
npm install -g package-name
```

--------------------------------

### Single code block example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of a single code block in JavaScript.

```javascript
const apiConfig = {
  baseURL: "https://api.example.com",
  timeout: 5000,
  headers: {
    Authorization: `Bearer ${process.env.API_TOKEN}`,
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies (macOS)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Commands to install Node.js and a package on macOS using Homebrew.

```bash
brew install node
npm install -g package-name
```

--------------------------------

### Configure environment step

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of configuring environment variables within a step.

```bash
API_KEY=your_api_key_here
```

--------------------------------

### Example Request-context Call

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/api.html

An example of a GET request to retrieve a file system tree, specifying a path, directory, and workspace.

```HTTP
GET /api/fs/tree?path=.&directory=/repo/app&workspace=ws_123
```

--------------------------------

### Required YAML frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of the YAML frontmatter required at the beginning of every documentation page.

```yaml
---
title: "Clear, specific, keyword-rich title"
description: "Concise description explaining page purpose and value"
---
```

--------------------------------

### Install SDK

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

Install the opencode SDK from npm.

```bash
npm install @opencode-ai/sdk
```

--------------------------------

### Example .windsurf/rules.md file

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/windsurf.mdx

A markdown file defining workspace rules for Windsurf's AI assistant, providing context about the Mintlify documentation project and writing standards. This file should be created in your project root.

```markdown
# Mintlify technical writing rule

## Project context

- This is a documentation project on the Mintlify platform
- We use MDX files with YAML frontmatter
- Navigation is configured in `docs.json`
- We follow technical writing best practices

## Writing standards

- Use second person ("you") for instructions
- Write in active voice and present tense
- Start procedures with prerequisites
- Include expected outcomes for major steps
- Use descriptive, keyword-rich headings
- Keep sentences concise but informative

## Required page structure

Every page must start with frontmatter:

```yaml
---
title: "Clear, specific title"
description: "Concise description for SEO and navigation"
---
```

## Mintlify components

### Callouts

- `<Note>` for helpful supplementary information
- `<Warning>` for important cautions and breaking changes
- `<Tip>` for best practices and expert advice
- `<Info>` for neutral contextual information
- `<Check>` for success confirmations

### Code examples

- When appropriate, include complete, runnable examples
- Use `<CodeGroup>` for multiple language examples
- Specify language tags on all code blocks
- Include realistic data, not placeholders
- Use `<RequestExample>` and `<ResponseExample>` for API docs

### Procedures

- Use `<Steps>` component for sequential instructions
- Include verification steps with `<Check>` components when relevant
- Break complex procedures into smaller steps

### Content organization

- Use `<Tabs>` for platform-specific content
- Use `<Accordion>` for progressive disclosure
- Use `<Card>` and `<CardGroup>` for highlighting content
- Wrap images in `<Frame>` components with descriptive alt text

## API documentation requirements

- Document all parameters with `<ParamField>`
- Show response structure with `<ResponseField>`
- Include both success and error examples
- Use `<Expandable>` for nested object properties
- Always include authentication examples

## Quality standards

- Test all code examples before publishing
- Use relative paths for internal links
- Include alt text for all images
- Ensure proper heading hierarchy (start with h2)
- Check existing patterns for consistency

```

--------------------------------

### Example Request Flow

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/llm/AGENTS.md

Demonstrates how to construct an LLM request using `LLM.request` and generate a response using `LLMClient.generate`.

```ts
const request = LLM.request({
  model: OpenAI.configure({ apiKey }).responses("gpt-4o-mini"),
  system: "You are concise.",
  prompt: "Say hello.",
})

const response = yield * LLMClient.generate(request)
```

--------------------------------

### InstanceState Initializer Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/specs/effect/guide.md

Demonstrates how to put subscriptions, finalizers, and scoped background work inside the InstanceState.make(...) initializer.

```ts
const cache =
  yield *
  InstanceState.make<State>(
    Effect.fn("Foo.state")(function* () {
      const bus = yield* Bus.Service

      yield* bus.subscribeAll().pipe(
        Stream.runForEach((event) => handleEvent(event)),
        Effect.forkScoped,
      )

      yield* Effect.acquireRelease(openResource, closeResource)

      return yield* loadInitialState()
    }),
  )
```

--------------------------------

### Install a plugin

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Install a plugin and update your config.

```bash
opencode plugin <module>
```

--------------------------------

### Install a plugin (alias)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Use the alias to install a plugin.

```bash
opencode plug <module>
```

--------------------------------

### Navigate to project directory

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Change directory to your project.

```bash
cd /path/to/project
```

--------------------------------

### OpenCode JSONC Configuration Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx

Illustrates the basic structure of an OpenCode configuration file, supporting JSON with comments, including schema, model, autoupdate, and server settings.

```jsonc
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5",
  "autoupdate": true,
  "server": {
    "port": 4096,
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode using Docker

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Run OpenCode using Docker.

```bash
docker run -it --rm ghcr.io/anomalyco/opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize OpenCode for project

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Run the /init command to analyze your project and create AGENTS.md.

```bash
/init
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode using Homebrew

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on macOS and Linux using Homebrew.

```bash
brew install anomalyco/tap/opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Example tui.json Configuration

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

A sample `tui.json` file demonstrating various configuration options for customizing TUI behavior.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/tui.json",
  "theme": "opencode",
  "leader_timeout": 2000,
  "keybinds": {
    "leader": "ctrl+x",
    "command_list": "ctrl+p"
  },
  "scroll_speed": 3,
  "scroll_acceleration": {
    "enabled": false
  },
  "diff_style": "auto",
  "mouse": true,
  "attention": {
    "enabled": true,
    "notifications": true,
    "sound": true,
    "volume": 0.4,
    "sound_pack": "opencode.default",
    "sounds": {
      "error": "./sounds/error.mp3"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on Windows using Mise

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on Windows using Mise.

```bash
mise use -g github:anomalyco/opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Session-pinned prompt call example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/api.html

An example of a session-pinned API call, showing the HTTP method, path, and how the server resolves the session context.

```HTTP
POST /api/session/ses_123/prompt

// server resolves
sessionID -> { directory, workspaceID? }
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Model Instructions in opencode.json

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx

Provide an array of paths or glob patterns to instruction files in the `instructions` option, guiding the model's behavior and responses.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "instructions": ["CONTRIBUTING.md", "docs/guidelines.md", ".cursor/rules/*.md"]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode using Node.js package managers

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode using npm, Bun, pnpm, or Yarn.

```bash
npm install -g opencode-ai
```

```bash
bun install -g opencode-ai
```

```bash
pnpm install -g opencode-ai
```

```bash
yarn global add opencode-ai
```

--------------------------------

### Example TUI Configuration File (tui.json)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx

This JSON snippet demonstrates a `tui.json` file for configuring OpenCode TUI-specific settings, including scroll behavior, diff style, mouse support, and attention notifications. The `$schema` property enables editor validation and autocompletion.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/tui.json",
  "scroll_speed": 3,
  "scroll_acceleration": {
    "enabled": true
  },
  "diff_style": "auto",
  "mouse": true,
  "attention": {
    "enabled": true,
    "notifications": true,
    "sound": true,
    "volume": 0.4
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on Windows using Scoop

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on Windows using Scoop.

```bash
scoop install opencode
```

--------------------------------

### GET /zen/go/v1/models

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/go.mdx

This endpoint allows fetching the full list of available AI models and their metadata.

```APIDOC
## GET /zen/go/v1/models

### Description
This endpoint allows fetching the full list of available AI models and their metadata.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/zen/go/v1/models
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on Windows using NPM

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on Windows using NPM.

```bash
npm install -g opencode-ai
```

--------------------------------

### Editor Setup

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Commands to set the `EDITOR` environment variable for different operating systems and shells, including how to use the `--wait` flag for GUI editors to block until closed.

```bash
# Example for nano or vim
export EDITOR=nano
export EDITOR=vim

# For GUI editors, VS Code, Cursor, VSCodium, Windsurf, Zed, etc.
# include --wait
export EDITOR="code --wait"
```

```cmd
set EDITOR=notepad

# For GUI editors, VS Code, Cursor, VSCodium, Windsurf, Zed, etc.
# include --wait
set EDITOR=code --wait
```

```powershell
$env:EDITOR = "notepad"

# For GUI editors, VS Code, Cursor, VSCodium, Windsurf, Zed, etc.
# include --wait
$env:EDITOR = "code --wait"
```

--------------------------------

### Executing a slash command

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Example of using a slash command in the OpenCode TUI.

```bash
/help
```

--------------------------------

### ApiEvent Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/api.html

An example of a 'message.part.delta' event using the ApiEvent envelope structure.

```json
{
  "id": "evt_01",
  "type": "message.part.delta",
  "time": 1760000000000,
  "context": {
    "directory": "/repo/app",
    "workspaceID": "ws_123"
  },
  "payload": {
    "sessionID": "ses_123",
    "messageID": "msg_456",
    "partID": "part_789",
    "field": "text",
    "delta": "hello"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Running a Bash command

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Example of executing a shell command directly from the TUI using the '!' prefix.

```bash
!ls -la
```

### new command

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Command to start a new session.

```bash
/new
```

### Install OpenCode on Arch Linux

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on Arch Linux using pacman or paru.

```bash
sudo pacman -S opencode
```

```bash
paru -S opencode-bin
```

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/claude-code.mdx

Command to install the Claude Code CLI tool using npm.

```bash
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

### Run Development Server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/README.md

Command to start the local development server for Mintlify documentation.

```bash
mint dev
```

--------------------------------

### Route Composition Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/llm/AGENTS.md

Example of composing a route using `Route.make(...)` for an OpenAI chat endpoint.

```ts
export const route = Route.make({
  id: "openai-chat",
  provider: "openai",
  protocol: OpenAIChat.protocol,
  endpoint: Endpoint.path("/chat/completions", {
    baseURL: "https://api.openai.com/v1",
  }),
  auth: Auth.bearer(),
  framing: Framing.sse,
})
```

--------------------------------

### Runtime Flags Test Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/specs/effect/guide.md

Example of how to vary runtime flags in tests using explicit layer variants.

```ts
const it = testEffect(MyService.defaultLayer.pipe(Layer.provide(RuntimeFlags.layer({ experimentalScout: true }))))
```

--------------------------------

### Ask OpenCode a question

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Example of asking OpenCode to explain a part of the codebase.

```txt
@packages/functions/src/api/index.ts
How is authentication handled in @packages/functions/src/api/index.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Referencing a configured alias

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/tui.mdx

Example of using a configured alias to reference a file in a TUI message.

```text
Compare our setup with @docs/README.md
```

--------------------------------

### Error Definition Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/specs/effect/guide.md

Example of defining a tagged error class, a domain-level error union, and its usage in a service interface.

```ts
export class SessionBusyError extends Schema.TaggedErrorClass<SessionBusyError>()("SessionBusyError", {
  sessionID: SessionID,
  message: Schema.String,
}) {}

export type Error = Storage.Error | SessionBusyError

export interface Interface {
  readonly get: (id: SessionID) => Effect.Effect<Info, Error>
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on Windows using Chocolatey

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on Windows using Chocolatey.

```bash
choco install opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Describe the feature in Plan mode

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Example of describing a new feature to OpenCode in Plan mode.

```txt
When a user deletes a note, we'd like to flag it as deleted in the database.
Then create a screen that shows all the recently deleted notes.
From this screen, the user can undelete a note or permanently delete it.
```

--------------------------------

### Config API Get and Providers

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

Examples of getting configuration information using `config.get()` and listing providers and default models using `config.providers()`.

```javascript
const config = await client.config.get()

const { providers, default: defaults } = await client.config.providers()
```

--------------------------------

### Paste API key

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Example of the prompt to paste your API key.

```txt
┌ API key
│
│
└ enter
```

--------------------------------

### Disable Automatic Updates in opencode.json

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/config.mdx

Set `autoupdate` to `false` in `opencode.json` to prevent OpenCode from automatically downloading new updates upon startup. This option does not apply if OpenCode was installed via a package manager.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "autoupdate": false
}
```

--------------------------------

### Required page frontmatter example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/windsurf.mdx

Example of the YAML frontmatter required at the beginning of every documentation page, as specified in the workspace rules.

```yaml
---
title: "Clear, specific title"
description: "Concise description for SEO and navigation"
---
```

--------------------------------

### Install Mintlify CLI

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/README.md

Command to install the Mintlify CLI globally using npm.

```bash
npm i -g mint
```

--------------------------------

### Partial Service Stub Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Example of using Layer.mock to create a partial service stub, overriding only necessary methods.

```typescript
import { Effect, Layer } from "effect"
import { Account } from "@/account/account"

const failingAccountLayer = Layer.mock(Account.Service, {
  orgsByAccount: () => Effect.fail(new Account.AccountServiceError({ message: "simulated upstream failure" })),
})
```

--------------------------------

### OpenAPI Configuration Examples

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/essentials/settings.mdx

Examples demonstrating different ways to configure the 'openapi' property.

```json
"openapi": "https://example.com/openapi.json"
```

```json
"openapi": "/openapi.json"
```

```json
"openapi": ["https://example.com/openapi1.json", "/openapi2.json", "/openapi3.json"]
```

--------------------------------

### tmpdir with Git repository

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Example of initializing a temporary directory as a Git repository.

```typescript
await using tmp = await tmpdir({ git: true })
```

--------------------------------

### Reviewing a Pull Request

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/src/command/template/review.txt

Commands to get context and diff for a pull request.

```bash
gh pr view $ARGUMENTS
```

```bash
gh pr diff $ARGUMENTS
```

--------------------------------

### Create and manage sessions

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

Examples demonstrating how to create, list, prompt, and inject context into sessions using the SDK.

```javascript
// Create and manage sessions
const session = await client.session.create({
  body: { title: "My session" },
})

const sessions = await client.session.list()

// Send a prompt message
const result = await client.session.prompt({
  path: { id: session.id },
  body: {
    model: { providerID: "anthropic", modelID: "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022" },
    parts: [{ type: "text", text: "Hello!" }],
  },
})

// Inject context without triggering AI response (useful for plugins)
await client.session.prompt({
  path: { id: session.id },
  body: {
    noReply: true,
    parts: [{ type: "text", text: "You are a helpful assistant." }],
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Path API Get Current Path

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

Example of using `path.get()` to retrieve current path information.

```javascript
// Get current path information
const pathInfo = await client.path.get()
```

--------------------------------

### Run the project

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/README.md

Command to run the project's main script using Bun.

```bash
bun run index.ts
```

--------------------------------

### tmpdir with config file

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Example of creating a temporary directory with a pre-configured `opencode.json` file.

```typescript
await using tmp = await tmpdir({
  config: { model: "test/model", username: "testuser" },
})
```
